description:
You are the on-call Site Reliability Engineer.  
A web-server access log already exists at:

    /home/user/logs/webserver.log

Your job is to create a concise report of every downtime incident.  
Follow these precise requirements:

1. Definition of a downtime line  
   • Any log line that contains the exact text “status=DOWN” (case-sensitive).  
   • No other lines qualify.

2. Destination report  
   • Path: /home/user/reports/downtime_summary.log  
   • If the directory /home/user/reports does not yet exist, create it.  
   • Overwrite the file if it already exists.

3. Exact output format of /home/user/reports/downtime_summary.log  
   • One line per downtime incident, in the same order the lines appear in the original log (i.e., chronological ascending for the provided file).  
   • Each line must contain exactly three whitespace-separated fields:  
        a. The ISO-8601 timestamp that begins the source log line (for example 2024-04-12T10:15:32Z)  
        b. The hostname from the second field of the source log line (for example server1.example.com)  
        c. The constant string DOWN (all capitals)  
   • Example line:  
        2024-04-12T10:15:32Z server1.example.com DOWN  
   • Do NOT include any other columns such as “status=” or “response_ms”.  
   • End the file with a single trailing newline and no extra blank lines.

4. Console output requirement  
   • After you finish creating downtime_summary.log, print only the total count of downtime events to STDOUT.  
   • The message must follow the exact pattern:  
        Total downtime events: <NUMBER>  
     Example:  
        Total downtime events: 4

The automated grader will verify BOTH:  
• The exact byte-for-byte contents of /home/user/reports/downtime_summary.log  
• The exact console line that states the count.

Use any standard shell tools (grep, awk, sed, etc.). You should be able to complete the task with no more than 3-4 elementary commands.  
Remember: do not display any additional output besides the required count line.
truth:
Initial filesystem state supplied to the agent:

Directory structure:
- /home/user/logs/            (already exists, 0755)
- /home/user/reports/         (does NOT exist yet)

File: /home/user/logs/webserver.log  (0644)  
Contents (8 lines, each terminated by '\n'):

2024-04-12T10:14:01Z server1.example.com status=UP   response_ms=101
2024-04-12T10:15:32Z server1.example.com status=DOWN response_ms=0
2024-04-12T10:16:45Z server2.example.com status=UP   response_ms=95
2024-04-12T10:17:02Z server2.example.com status=DOWN response_ms=0
2024-04-12T10:18:01Z server1.example.com status=UP   response_ms=103
2024-04-12T10:19:20Z server1.example.com status=DOWN response_ms=0
2024-04-12T10:20:45Z server2.example.com status=UP   response_ms=96
2024-04-12T10:21:11Z server3.example.com status=DOWN response_ms=0

Expected file created by the agent:

Path: /home/user/reports/downtime_summary.log  (0644)  
Expected contents (exact, including final newline):

2024-04-12T10:15:32Z server1.example.com DOWN
2024-04-12T10:17:02Z server2.example.com DOWN
2024-04-12T10:19:20Z server1.example.com DOWN
2024-04-12T10:21:11Z server3.example.com DOWN

Expected STDOUT from the last command the agent runs:

Total downtime events: 4
name: task_000000_e750087d
